Name: Alameda
What_to_order: Origami tasting menu (12 courses), Ria Formosa oysters, octopus from Olhao
Notes: Chef Rui Sequeira. Faro's first Michelin star. Modern Algarve cuisine. Vegetal menu available.

Name: Ocean Restaurant
Notes: Chef Hans Neuner. Austria-meets-Algarve. Since 2011. Worth the 45min drive from Faro.

Name: Vila Joya
Notes: Two stars since 1999. Cliffside. One of Portugal's most iconic fine dining rooms.

Name: Bon Bon
Notes: Chef Louis Anjos. Celebrates Algarve diversity. 40min from Faro.

Name: Vista Restaurant
Notes: Chef Joao Oliveira. Overlooks Praia da Rocha. Star since 2017.

Name: Gusto by Heinz Beck
Notes: Three-star Rome chef's Algarve outpost. Polished service. Hotel dining.

Name: CHECKin by Leonel Pereira
What_to_order: Tasting menu, Algarve seasonal produce, wine pairings
Notes: Former Michelin-starred chef who gave up his star for accessibility. Converted storehouse. Strong wine list.

Name: Restaurante Cidade Velha
What_to_order: Cataplana de marisco, fried octopus, seafood rice
Notes: At the foot of Faro Cathedral. Traditional with quality. Terrace dining.

Name: Tasca do Ricky
Notes: Warm hospitality. Not fancy. Genuinely authentic Algarve flavours.

Name: O Recife
What_to_order: Piri-piri chicken (grilled over charcoal), grilled fish
Notes: Smoky grilled everything. Portuguese comfort food done right.

Name: A Do Pinto
What_to_order: Cataplana with shrimp and clams, grilled fish
Notes: Family-run. Honest portions. Classic Algarvian cataplana.

Name: Tertulia Algarvia
Notes: Also runs cooking classes. Good place to understand Algarve cuisine.

Name: Vila Adentro
Notes: 18th-century building. The rare place that does vegetarian cataplana well.

Name: O Chavalar
Notes: Mainly fish. Very local. Good value.

Name: O Alminhas
Notes: Very local. Budget-friendly. No frills.

Name: Restaurante Ria Formosa
What_to_order: Seafood cataplana, grilled catch of the day
Notes: Waterfront. 15min from Faro. Worth the trip for fresher catch.

Name: O Horta
What_to_order: Seafood feijoada, pork with clams, cataplana
Notes: Facing Ria Formosa. Extensive menu. Regional seafood specialist.

Name: Grupo Naval de Olhao (G.N.O.)
What_to_order: Seafood cataplana, grilled fish, percebes when available
Notes: Edge of Ria Formosa. Marina views. Club atmosphere.

Name: A Taska
Notes: Cozy. Authentic flavours. Honest.

Name: Estamine
What_to_order: Fresh oysters, grilled fish, arroz de lingueirao
Notes: Desert island restaurant. Boat access only. Eco-friendly. One of the most singular dining experiences in the Algarve.

Name: Bifanas de Vendas Novas
What_to_order: Bifana (pork sandwich), soup + bifana + drink combo
Notes: Meal deal under EUR 5. Fast. No-nonsense.

Name: Roberto's Chicken Piri-Piri
Notes: Charcoal-grilled chicken with proper piri-piri.

Name: Bago Wine Bar
What_to_order: Natural wines by the glass, Portuguese small producers
Notes: Good natural wine selection. Music. Relaxed.

Name: Hotel Faro Rooftop
Notes: Best rooftop view in Faro. Harbour and Ria Formosa panorama.

Name: Rooftop Eva
Notes: Pool. Young crowd. Sunset parties in summer.

Name: Columbus
Notes: DJ sets Wed-Sat. Live music Sunday. Restaurant-bar hybrid.

Name: 24 Bar Faro
Notes: Student energy. Gets going late. Indie and electronic music.

Name: Palimpsestu Art Bar
Notes: Art bar with DJ sets. Funk and indie nights.

Name: O Castelo
Notes: Fado performances on Mondays. Inside the old walls.

Name: Guess?
Notes: Fado to hard rock. Free entry most events.

Name: Mercado Municipal de Faro
Notes: Central market. Go early. Saturday is busiest.

Name: Mercado de Olhao
What_to_order: Fresh fish, sardines (EUR 4/kg), fruit, vegetables, meat
Notes: Two halls: fish + produce. Since 1916. 15min from Faro. The real market experience.

Name: Morgado do Quintao
What_to_order: Negra Mole wines, wine tasting under 2000-year-old olive tree
Notes: Joana Macanita winemaking. Low-intervention. Indigenous varieties. Farmstay available.

Name: Cabrita Wines
Notes: Family project since 1977. 16ha across 3 properties. Algarve terroir.

Name: Quinta dos Vales
Notes: 44ha estate. Sculpture garden. Wine hotel.

Name: Quinta do Barranco Longo
Notes: Founded 2001. Clay-limestone soils. National and international varieties.

Name: Cafe Alianca
Notes: Old-world charm. WiFi. Good for a morning start.

Name: Cowork Algarve
Notes: Dedicated workspace. Private offices and shared desks.

Name: Traditional pastelarias
What_to_order: Dom Rodrigos, morgadinhos, queijo de figo, doce fino
Notes: Convent-origin sweets. Egg yolk + almond base. Buy in Tavira or Lagos for best quality.
